spoken: The dashboard gives you an instant view of everything happening across your investor accounts — what's been cleared, what needs attention, and any exceptions that have come in.
Operators can navigate the entire system without touching the mouse — designed for high-volume environments.
G then T for Transfers, G then R for the Review Queue, G then E for Settlement — you're never more than two keystrokes from anywhere you need to be.
Control-K opens a command palette that searches any transfer or account — from anywhere in the application.
Here we're submitting a deposit with real check images — front and back — just as an investor would upload them from a mobile device.
Recent deposits appear below the form instantly, with live status updates as each one processes.
Every check goes through AI analysis — image quality, the printed dollar amount, routing number, and account information are all verified automatically on every submission.
One submission. Automated analysis. Compliance checks. Accounting entries. All in seconds.
The status updates in real time — you can watch the deposit move through each stage without refreshing the page.
Funds posted. The deposit passed all checks and the investor's account has been credited.
Five compliance checks ran automatically — account eligibility, deposit limits, contribution type, and duplicate detection. Every one passed.
If this check bounces later, a return can be initiated in one click — with automatic fee calculation and accounting.
This check shows seven hundred fifty dollars, but the customer declared five hundred. The system catches that discrepancy automatically and routes it for human review.
Flagged for review. No manual monitoring required — the system caught it.
The review queue shows every deposit waiting for an operator decision — with how long each item has been waiting.
In the review detail, the operator sees the full transfer information and the check images together.
The operator can compare the front and back images directly against what the AI reported.
The AI read the printed amount as seven fifty. That discrepancy is what triggered the review.
Every action is recorded — state changes, who took each action, and when. Full audit trail, built in.
After reviewing the images and the AI's findings, the operator approves the deposit with a note.
Approved. The deposit clears and the investor's account is credited immediately.
Settlement packages all cleared deposits into the industry-standard clearing file format required by the Federal Reserve.
This is the actual format used by US banks to exchange checks — with the check images embedded, as required.
Settlement file generated and ready for transmission to the clearing network.
Acknowledge confirms that the clearing bank has received and accepted the settlement file.
Settlement complete. All deposits in this batch are now fully settled.
When a check bounces, the return is processed using the bank's standard return reason code.
Type the first few characters of a transfer ID and press Tab to auto-complete it.
NSF — Non-Sufficient Funds — the most common reason for returned checks.
The system reverses the original deposit and applies the non-sufficient funds fee — automatically.
Return processed. The original deposit is reversed and the NSF fee is recorded against the investor's account.
Every transaction flows through the ledger — investor accounts, clearing accounts, and fee revenue — all reconciled.
The complete picture: the original deposit, the return reversal, and the NSF fee. Every debit matched by a credit.
Four core workflows — from submission to settlement to returns — all in one platform.
Live breakdown by status. Click any row to filter the transfers view instantly.
